title: One-step multiplication & division equations: fractions & decimals
description: Practice solving equations in one step by multiplying or dividing a number from both sides. These problems involve decimals and fractions.
topic_path: One-step multiplication & division equations: fractions & decimals

hints:
We need to get $a$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $2$, the reciprocal of $\dfrac{1}{2}$:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac12a &=11
\\\\
\purpleD{2 \cdot} \dfrac12a &=11 \purpleD{\cdot 2}
\\\\
1a &=22
\\\\
a &= 22
\end{align}$
The solution is $a=22$.

We need to get $y$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $\dfrac{5}{3}$, the reciprocal of $\dfrac{3}{5}$:

$\begin{align}
6&=\dfrac{3}{5}y
\\\\
\purpleD{\dfrac{5}{3}\cdot }6&=\purpleD{\dfrac{5}{3}\cdot }\dfrac{3}{5}y
\\\\
\dfrac{30}{3}&=1y \\\\
10&=y
\end{align}$
The solution is $y=10$.

We need to get $p$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $\dfrac{3}{2}$, the reciprocal of $\dfrac{2}{3}$:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{2}{3}p &=5 \\\\
\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{2}\cdot}\dfrac{2}{3}p &=\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{2}\cdot}5 \\\\
1p &= \dfrac{15}{2} \\\\
p &= \dfrac{15}{2}
\end{align}$
The solution is $p=\dfrac{15}{2}$.

We need to get $x$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $\dfrac{1}{4}$, the reciprocal of $4$:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{3}{2}&=4x \\\\
\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}\cdot}\dfrac{3}{2}&=\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}\cdot}4x \\\\
\dfrac{3}{8} &= 1x \\\\
\dfrac{3}{8} &= x
\end{align}$
The solution is $x=\dfrac{3}{8}$.

We need to get $d$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $\dfrac{1}{5}$, the reciprocal of $5$:

$\begin{align}
5d&=\dfrac{1}{2} \\\\
\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{5}\cdot}5d&=\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{5}\cdot}\dfrac{1}{2} \\\\
1d &= \dfrac{1}{10} \\\\
d &= \dfrac{1}{10}
\end{align}$
The solution is $d=\dfrac{1}{10}$.

We need to get $x$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $\dfrac{3}{2}$, the reciprocal of $\dfrac{2}{3}$:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{2} &=\dfrac{2}{3}x \\\\
\dfrac{1}{2} \purpleD{\cdot\dfrac{3}{2}}&=\dfrac{2}{3}x  \purpleD{\cdot\dfrac{3}{2}}\\\\
\dfrac{3}{4} &= 1x \\\\
\dfrac{3}{4} &= x
\end{align}$
The solution is $x=\dfrac{3}{4}$.

We need to get $b$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $\dfrac{3}{4}$, the reciprocal of $\dfrac{4}{3}$:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{4}{3}b&=\dfrac{4}{5} \\\\
\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}\cdot}\dfrac{4}{3}b &=\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}\cdot}\dfrac{4}{5} \\\\
1b &=\dfrac{3\cdot \cancel{4}}{\cancel{4}\cdot 5} \\\\
b&=\dfrac{3}{5} 
\end{align}$
The solution is $b=\dfrac{3}{5}$.

We need to get $w$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $\dfrac{6}{5}$, the reciprocal of $\dfrac{5}{6}$:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{5}{6}w &=\dfrac{10}{3} \\\\
\purpleD{\dfrac{6}{5}\cdot}\dfrac{5}{6}w &=\purpleD{\dfrac{6}{5}\cdot}\dfrac{10}{3} \\\\
1w &= \dfrac{60}{15} \\\\
w &= 4
\end{align}$
The solution is $w=4$.

We need to get $h$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is $1$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can multiply both sides of the equation by $\dfrac{10}{7}$, the reciprocal of $\dfrac{7}{10}$:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{7}{2} &=\dfrac{7}{10}h \\\\
\purpleD{\dfrac{10}{7}\cdot }\dfrac{7}{2} &=\purpleD{\dfrac{10}{7}\cdot }\dfrac{7}{10}h \\\\
\dfrac{70}{14} &= 1h \\\\
5 &= h
\end{align}$
The solution is $h=5$.
We need to get $g$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Multiplying both sides of the equation by $4$ gives us:

$\begin{align} 
\dfrac{g}4 &=3.2 \\\\
\dfrac{g}4\purpleD{\cdot 4}  &= 3.2\purpleD{\cdot 4} \\\\
g &= 12.8
\end{align}$
The solution is $g=12.8$.
We need to get $n$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Dividing both sides of the equation by $12$ gives us:

$\begin{align} 
3.6&=12n \\\\
\dfrac{3.6}{\purpleD{12}} &=\dfrac{12n}{\purpleD{12}} \\\\
0.3&=n 
\end{align}$
The solution is $n=0.3$.
We need to get $t$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Dividing both sides of the equation by $0.8$ gives us:

$\begin{align} 
0.8t&=4 \\\\
\dfrac{0.8t}{\purpleD{0.8}} &= \dfrac{4}{\purpleD{0.8}} \\\\
t&=5
\end{align}$
The solution is $t=5$.
We need to get $x$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Multiplying both sides of the equation by $0.3$ gives us:

$\begin{align} 
40&=\dfrac{x}{0.3} \\\\
40 \purpleD{\cdot 0.3}&=\dfrac{x}{0.3} \purpleD{\cdot 0.3}\\\\
12&=x 
\end{align}$
The solution is $x=12$.
We need to get $b$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Dividing both sides of the equation by $0.3$ gives us:

$\begin{align}
0.3b &=0.9 \\\\
\dfrac{0.3b}{\purpleD{0.3}} &= \dfrac{0.9}{\purpleD{0.3}} \\\\
b &= 3
\end{align}$
The solution is $b=3$.
We need to get $w$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Multiplying both sides of the equation by $2.8$ gives us:

$\begin{align}
2.5&=\dfrac{w}{2.8} \\\\
2.5  \purpleD{\cdot 2.8}&=\dfrac{w}{2.8} \purpleD{\cdot 2.8} \\\\
7&=w 
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
The solution is $w=7$.
We need to get $k$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Dividing both sides of the equation by $1.5$ gives us:

$\begin{align}
1.2&=1.5k \\\\
 \dfrac{1.2}{\purpleD{1.5}} &=\dfrac{1.5k}{\purpleD{1.5}} \\\\
0.8&=k 
\end{align}$
The solution is $k=0.8$.
We need to get $r$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Multiplying both sides of the equation by $0.4$ gives us:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{r}{0.4} &=1.5 \\\\
\dfrac{r}{0.4} \purpleD{\cdot 0.4}&=1.5  \purpleD{\cdot 0.4}\\\\
r &= 0.6
\end{align}$
The solution is $r=0.6$.
We need to get $t$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Dividing both sides of the equation by $4$ gives us:

$\begin{align}
9 &= 4t \\\\
\dfrac{9}{\purpleD{4}} &= \dfrac{4t}{\purpleD{4}} \\\\
\dfrac{9}{4} &= t &\text{Fraction}\\\\
2.25 &= t &\text{Decimal}
\end{align}$
The solution is $t=\dfrac{9}{4}$ or $t=2.25$.
We need to get $c$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Dividing both sides of the equation by $5$ gives us:

$\begin{align}
5c &= 13 \\\\
\dfrac{5c}{\purpleD{5}} &= \dfrac{13}{\purpleD{5}} \\\\
c &= \dfrac{13}{5} &\text{Fraction}\\\\
c &= 2.6 &\text{Decimal}
\end{align}$
The solution is $c=\dfrac{13}{5}$ or $c=2.6$.
We need to get $j$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Dividing both sides of the equation by $12$ gives us:

$\begin{align}
12j &= 21 \\\\
\dfrac{12j}{\purpleD{12}} &= \dfrac{21}{\purpleD{12}} \\\\
j &= \dfrac{\cancel{3}\cdot 7}{\cancel{3}\cdot 4} \\\\
j &= \dfrac{7}{4} &\text{Fraction}\\\\
j &= 1.75 &\text{Decimal}
\end{align}$
The solution is $j=\dfrac{7}{4}$ or $j=1.75$.
We need to get $v$ by itself by performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.

Dividing both sides of the equation by $7$ gives us:

$\begin{align}
11 &= 7v \\\\
\dfrac{11}{\purpleD{7}} &= \dfrac{7v}{\purpleD{7}} \\\\
\dfrac{11}{7} &= v
\end{align}$
The solution is $v=\dfrac{11}{7}$.
